repos.name: rhdh-templates
repos.description: RHDH scaffolder templates for bootstrapping new services via the internal developer portal.

repos.name: example-api
repos.description: FastAPI reference template with DNS and FQDN endpoints — the starting point for new platform APIs.

repos.name: apis-library
repos.description: Shared Python library with async connectors for AWX, ArgoCD, Bitbucket, and Vault, plus a reusable FastAPI application factory.

repos.name: aws-terraform-bootstrap
repos.description: One-time Terraform that creates the shared S3 bucket used for Terragrunt remote state across all projects.

repos.name: aws-xray-db-sync
repos.description: Lambda that runs every minute to sync JFrog Xray offline DB bundles to S3, with SNS alerts on success or failure.

repos.name: SCP blocks ALB/NLB → Cloudflare Tunnel
repos.description: An SCP pushed down from the Control Tower management account denies elasticloadbalancing:CreateLoadBalancer in member accounts, so provisioning an ALB/NLB fails outright. Workaround: skip the load balancer entirely and expose the service via a Cloudflare Tunnel — it also hands you free DNS and a managed TLS certificate, without opening any inbound ports.

repos.name: SCP blocks IAM OIDC Identity Provider
repos.description: An SCP pushed down from the Control Tower management account denies iam:CreateOpenIDConnectProvider in member accounts, blocking OIDC federation (e.g. for GitHub Actions) at the account level — the identity provider has to be created from the management account instead, since no IAM policy change inside the member account can override an SCP.

repos.name: SCP blocks Route53 CreateHostedZone
repos.description: An SCP pushed down from the Control Tower management account denies route53:CreateHostedZone in member accounts, so a private hosted zone for internal DNS (e.g. giving an EC2 instance a stable name that survives replacement) fails outright — no IAM policy inside the member account can override an SCP explicit deny. Workaround: have Terraform publish the dynamic value (e.g. the instance's private IP) to an SSM Parameter Store parameter on every apply, and have consumers read it from SSM instead of resolving a DNS name.
